title: Lindernia pygmaea
taxonomy: Lindernia pygmaea is classified within the family Linderniaceae. This family is part of the order Lamiales, which consists of various flowering plants. Lindernia pygmaea is native to Australia and is often found in wetland areas. It is a type of aquatic or semi-aquatic plant, characterized by its small size and delicate appearance. The exact classification of Lindernia pygmaea can be summarized as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Eudicots, clade Asterids, order Lamiales, family Linderniaceae, and genus Lindernia.

Its leaves and stems are said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be used to treat various ailments. In traditional medicine, Lindernia pygmaea is often used to treat digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems.
